title: sambucus mexicana subsp. velutina
intro: Sambucus mexicana subsp. velutina, also known as the velvety elderberry, is a subspecies of the elderberry plant (Sambucus) native to Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Sambucus mexicana subsp. velutina include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever and inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
